replication_study.hypothesis: There will be a statistically-significant Level (Category-only vs. Brand) × Product Type (Utilitarian vs. Symbolic) interaction on the benefit-minus-symbolism attitude difference score: utilitarian products will become relatively more symbolic (smaller difference) when evaluated at the Brand level, whereas symbolic products will become relatively more utilitarian (larger difference) when evaluated at the Brand level.

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single 2020 data-collection wave; we retain respondents that pass both attention checks and screen-out repeats (see script filters).
data_plan.sample_size: ≈200 participants after exclusions (initial N = 637 rows in LeBeouf_replication_data.csv). Final N printed by the script).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participant (ID); repeated measures across 8 products.

qualification.explanation: The dataset was collected independently (2020 online sample) but reproduces the same experimental design, stimuli list, and measures, thus providing a direct replication on a new sample.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Uses the same 2×2 mixed design described in the original paper (post_registration.json → method/description; initial_details.txt → ANOVA interaction) and the identical 8 target products (ItemsList_Final.csv). Outcome variable (Benefit − Symbolism difference) and independent variables (Level, Product Type) match exactly.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original studies (post_registration.json → data/wave_or_subset) ran in-lab with U.S. undergraduates (2003–2009). Replication data were collected online (Qualtrics, 2020) with broader MTurk/Prolific style sample and slightly different brand name sets (Cond 3 vs 4 in ItemsList_Final.csv). Therefore respondents, timing, and brand lists form a novel sample while keeping the structure intact.

data_plan.notes: Two files are required: LeBeouf_replication_data.csv (raw responses) and ItemsList_Final.csv (item metadata). The raw file contains multiple util/symbol blocks for three brand-set conditions; script removes irrelevant columns per condition. Familiarity and bipolar scales are dropped to mirror original DV choice.

steps: Load raw Qualtrics CSV and item-metadata CSV.
Apply exclusion rules: Status==0, screenQ not NA and ==2, Attention1==7, Attention2==1.
Remove administration, familiarity, and bipolar columns.
Split data into NoBrand (Cond 1 & 2), Brand-A (Cond 3), Brand-B (Cond 4).
Reshape utilitarian and symbolic ratings from wide to long for each condition; label Condition (Category vs Brand).
Merge long data with ItemsList_Final to attach Category and ProductType (Utilitarian/Symbolic).
Collapse to participant × ProductType × Condition means.
Run 2(Level: between) × 2(ProductType: within) mixed ANOVA on ScoreDiff.
Report F-statistic, p-value, and partial η²; produce descriptive means and post-hoc tests.
planned_method.models: Mixed-effects ANOVA (Pingouin’s mixed_anova; within = ProductType, between = Condition, subject = ID).
planned_method.independent_variables: Condition (Category vs Brand), ProductType (Utilitarian vs Symbolic).
planned_method.control_variables: Stimulus set counterbalancing via Cond (1–4) as in original; random order inherent in survey design.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pandas, numpy, pingouin, statsmodels, scipy.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: F-statistic for the Level × ProductType interaction and partial η².
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Mixed ANOVA with Satterthwaite correction (Pingouin implementation).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion after exclusions (participants failing attention or missing critical fields removed).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Tukey-style (Bonferroni) adjustment for post-hoc pairwise t-tests (pg.pairwise_ttests padjust='bonf').
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replicate if interaction direction matches original and p < .05 (two-tailed) or effect size confidence interval excludes zero.

files.replication_data/replication_analysis.py: End-to-end Python script that cleans the data, reshapes to long format, merges with item metadata, computes ScoreDiff, saves tidy and collapsed datasets, performs the mixed ANOVA, prints results, and optional post-hoc comparisons.

codebase.notes: The script is a full Python rewrite of the provided R analysis (LeBoeuf_…_Final.R) to comply with execution requirements. All paths use /app/data. Generated outputs (tidy_long.csv, collapsed.csv) are stored in the same folder for transparency.

analysis.instructions: Inside the container with /app as workdir, run:  python /app/data/original/23/input/replication_data/replication_analysis.py
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare F(1,df) and p-value for Level × ProductType interaction against original Study 1b result (F(1,204)=7.70, p=.006) as documented in post_registration.json and initial_details.txt; also compare direction and partial η².
